text:
In a little town called Willow Creek lived a fox named Freddy. One day, while going through his usual routine chasing rodents and enjoying the forest views, he stumbled upon something beautiful. A field of sunflowers. Not just any field—here they were enormous birds' feathers. Freddy had never seen anything like it.

He plucked a few feathers. They were soft and felt nice. Perhaps he could make a hat? That afternoon, Freddy decorated an old hat he happened to have. When he put it on, it felt just like those feathers.

That night, dreaming of feathers, Freddy spoke as though the wind were there. "Oh soft things, maybe I'll flutter about like you."

The next morning, a soft wind blew through Willow Creek. Freddy felt gloriously feathered.

Throughout the week, he fluttered around. The children loved his new plumage. Even the squirrels seemed charmed.

By week's end, Freddy's feather hat was everywhere. Trees had them hanging from
